raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft makes a causal and comparative argument that would need checking for internal coherence as part of improvement.","R1.3":"The performance claims and broad conclusions depend on whether the reported experiments support them as written.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request can reasonably include section-level narrative flow and ordering.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation directly asks to improve the draft rather than evaluate a narrow technical issue.","RX.1":"Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough information to verify claims.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong comparative language, so avoiding overclaiming is an active cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The response must stay within improving the draft and not drift into unrelated experimental redesign or execution.","RX.4":"The draft concerns operational multi-agent control systems, so avoiding claims that could encourage unsafe downstream deployment is active.","RX.5":"Changes or recommendations should remain traceable to the supplied artifact and distinguish given facts from inferred improvements."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes a causal and comparative argument that would need checking for internal coherence as part of improvement.
rationale.R1.3: The performance claims and broad conclusions depend on whether the reported experiments support them as written.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request can reasonably include section-level narrative flow and ordering.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation directly asks to improve the draft rather than evaluate a narrow technical issue.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough information to verify claims.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong comparative language, so avoiding overclaiming is an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within improving the draft and not drift into unrelated experimental redesign or execution.
rationale.RX.4: The draft concerns operational multi-agent control systems, so avoiding claims that could encourage unsafe downstream deployment is active.
rationale.RX.5: Changes or recommendations should remain traceable to the supplied artifact and distinguish given facts from inferred improvements.
